query: In my Claude Code project I want the instruction 'validate every endpoint's input and use the standard error envelope' to load ONLY when Claude works with files under src/api/ (TypeScript). It must not sit in always-loaded memory. Tell me exactly which file to create and show its complete content including any frontmatter. Self-contained question — do not read the filesystem.
baseline_failure: RED: the agent puts a `paths:` block on CLAUDE.md itself (unsupported — CLAUDE.md loads by directory hierarchy only), or invents frontmatter shape (paths as a plain string / wrong key), or falls back to prose in CLAUDE.md like 'when working with API files...' which still always loads.
expected_behavior: Creates a rules file (e.g. .claude/rules/api.md), NOT CLAUDE.md
Frontmatter has paths: as a YAML LIST of glob items (e.g. - "src/api/**/*.ts")
States or implies the conditional-loading semantics: the rule loads when Claude reads a matching file
anti_expectation: Does NOT move ALL project instructions out of CLAUDE.md — only the path-scoped rule is conditional
Does NOT claim path-scoped rules can also live on CLAUDE.md via the same frontmatter

baseline_observed.method: 2 isolated haiku probes answering from model knowledge (no skill), then GREEN with the skill's golden-rules + conditional-rules sections in context

runs.note: hallucinated src/api/.claude.md file, 'no frontmatter needed' — wrong filename AND wrong mechanism
hallucinated frontmatter keys scope: + languages: — plausible-looking, entirely invented

baseline_observed.verdict: RED 2/2 — clean reproduce, and the failure is the dangerous kind: confidently-formatted invented config that a user would paste and silently get no conditional loading. This is the doc-verified skill's core value.

runs.note: .claude/rules/api-validation.md with paths: as quoted YAML list of globs — exact doc-correct shape, explains read-triggered loading
